edges.description: High-energy CNS tissues are vulnerable to complex IV failure, producing the Leigh-spectrum neurodevelopmental phenotype.

edges.description: Profound complex IV failure disrupts energy homeostasis across neonatal liver, muscle, kidney, and brain.

edges.description: Bioenergetic failure in deep gray matter produces the bilateral brainstem/basal ganglia lesions of Leigh syndrome.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Lactate overproduction manifests clinically as lactic acidosis.
Severe lactic acidosis is part of the neonatal metabolic decompensation that accompanies multiorgan energy failure.
CNS energy failure manifests as Leigh-spectrum neurodegeneration.
Leigh-spectrum CNS injury manifests clinically as seizures.
Chronic neurodevelopmental injury manifests as global developmental delay.
Motor-system involvement manifests as hypotonia.
Severe neurodevelopmental and metabolic disease contributes to failure to thrive.
Severe neurologic and metabolic involvement contributes to respiratory insufficiency.
Prenatal energy failure contributes to antenatal growth restriction.
Prenatal brain energy failure manifests as severe microcephaly.
Energy-metabolism failure manifests as neonatal hypoglycemia.
Renal tubular energy failure manifests as generalized aminoaciduria.
Hepatic energy failure manifests as decreased liver function.
Hepatic synthetic dysfunction manifests as prolonged prothrombin time.
Hepatic synthetic dysfunction manifests as hypoalbuminemia.
Skeletal muscle energy failure manifests as elevated creatine kinase.

nodes.description: Characteristic bilateral lesions of the brainstem and basal ganglia, the neuroradiological hallmark of Leigh syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A founder mutation in PET100 causes isolated complex IV deficiency in Lebanese individuals with Leigh syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Leigh syndrome (LS) is a severe neurodegenerative disorder with characteristic bilateral lesions, typically in the brainstem and basal ganglia.

nodes.description: Biallelic PET100 variants cause loss of a small mitochondrial inner-membrane Complex IV biogenesis factor. PET100 forms a subcomplex with Complex IV subunits and is required for their incorporation into the maturing holoenzyme; in its absence the COX subunits are destabilized and the holoenzyme fails to assemble, yielding isolated Complex IV biogenesis failure.
nodes.id: PET100 Loss and Failed Complex IV Assembly

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: A founder mutation in PET100 causes isolated complex IV deficiency in Lebanese individuals with Leigh syndrome.
evidence.snippet: C19orf79 was recently renamed PET100 and predicted to encode a complex IV biogenesis factor.

evidence.reference_title: A founder mutation in PET100 causes isolated complex IV deficiency in Lebanese individuals with Leigh syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We showed that it is located in the mitochondrial inner membrane and forms a ∼300 kDa subcomplex with complex IV subunits.

evidence.reference_title: A truncating PET100 variant causing fatal infantile lactic acidosis and isolated cytochrome c oxidase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Exome sequencing revealed a homozygous truncating variant (c.142C>T, p.(Gln48*)) in the PET100 gene that results in a complete loss of enzyme activity and assembly of the holocomplex.
